DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS DEFENDANT LITTLE BEE GROUP, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, SARRA HE, EVAN WALLITT DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Tomorrow (May 11, 2022) is the trial date for this case. Cross-defendants' demurrer to the second amended cross complaint (SACC) is overruled. Should the trial judge find the need to further amend the SACC, that may be done "at any stage of the proceeding." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.)

Rescission. The SAAC adequately pleads a claim for rescission. The at-issue written contract is the cross offer agreement. (SACC 4:25-26, 10:11.) Mistakes are pled. (See, e.g., SACC 10:12-11:6.) The parties to the contract are pled. (SACC 4:25-26.) Filing of a rescission claim is notice thereof; whether notice was sufficiently prompt and return of consideration was required are for adjudication. (Civ. Code 1691; Hil-Mac Corp. v. Mendo Wood Products, Inc. (1965) 235 Cal.App.2d 526, 530.)

Reformation. The SAAC adequately pleads a claim for reformation. The at-issue written contract is the cross offer agreement. (SACC 4:25-26, 10:11.) Mistakes are pled. (See, e.g., SACC 10:12-11:6.) The parties to the contract are pled. (SACC 4:25-26.) The contract's "real meaning" is pled. (See, e.g., SACC 3:9-19.) The relevance, if any, of the "2018 Notice" is for adjudication.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Strike
Off Calendar May-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS LITTLE BEE GROUP, INC., SARRA HE, EVAN WALLITT's Motion To Strike.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to strike the second amended cross complaint's (SACC) third cause of action (breach of fiduciary duty) is granted because it is unopposed. (Opp. 2:7-9.)

The second part of the motion to strike is denied for four reasons, each dispositive. First, the notice of motion misquotes the SACC language it would seek to strike. (Compare Not. 2:9-12 with SACC 10:17-19.) Second, other parts of the SACC that cross-defendants do not seek to strike plead effectively the same matter (see, e.g., id. at 3:9-19), so the motion would be superfluous. Third, the language from the prior order the motion relies on is dicta regarding the SACC third cause of action (breach of fiduciary duty) which is no longer at issue. Fourth, the dicta says a shareholder derivative suit was not filed, not that one was not threatened.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Defendants' Notice Of Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement Of Derivative Action
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeremy Sugerman, Esq. (415.828.1895; Sugerman@gkhs.com) of GKHS for Plaintiffs.
Michael Betz, Esq. (415.837.1515; mbetz@allenmatkins.com) of Allen Matkins for Defendants (to argue the motion for summary judgment).
Gregory de la Pena, Esq. (415.268.8000; gdelapena@dlphlaw.com) of DLPH for Defendants (to argue the motion for leave to file second amended cross complaint).

Not argued. Mr. de la Pena stated they are withdrawing their request to contest the motion for court approval of settlement.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MIRANDA PAN, SKY KIDS INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, TINKER PRESCHOOL, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, TINKER PRESCHOOL and COLE VALLEY INC.'S Of Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement Of Derivative Action.

Defendants' "Motion for Court Approval of Settlement of Derivative Action" is denied. In Robbins v. Alibrandi (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 438, 449, the court explains that a court reviews a derivative suit settlement to protect those who are not directly represented in the settlement negotiations. Here, there is no basis for this motion. First, there was never an underlying derivative action that settled. The parties entered into the alleged settlement prior to the commencement of any litigation. Second, as defendant concedes, all of the stakeholders were part of the settlement process so there is no need for the court to sanction the settlement to protect the rights of non-appearing parties. (Defendant's Points and Authorities, 5:2-3; 6:1-16.) The pending summary judgment motion or a trial-rather than this motion-is the vehicle to address whether the release bars plaintiff's present claims.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Jeremy Sugerman, Esq. (415.828.1895; Sugerman@gkhs.com) of GKHS for Plaintiffs.
Michael Betz, Esq. (415.837.1515; mbetz@allenmatkins.com) of Allen Matkins for Defendants (to argue the motion for summary judgment).
Gregory de la Pena, Esq. (415.268.8000; gdelapena@dlphlaw.com) of DLPH for Defendants (to argue the motion for leave to file second amended cross complaint).

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS MIRANDA PAN, SKY KIDS INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, TINKER PRESCHOOL, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, TINKER PRESCHOOL, COLE VALLEY INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross Complaint.

Defendants'/Cross-complainants' motion for leave to file second amended cross-complaint is denied without prejudice. The De La Pena declaration does not comply with CRC 3.1324(b). Movant fails to explain why the proposed amendment is necessary and proper and specifically when the facts arose that support the proposed amendments. Movant relies on the Wallett deposition testimony as grounds for the amendment, but he was deposed in November of 2020. (De La Pena Decl., Ex. C.) Allowing the amendment at this point-less than a month before trial when discovery has closed-would unduly prejudice plaintiff. The court denies the motion based on movant's undue delay and the prejudice to plaintiff. (See Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159 [affirming denial of leave to amend based on undue delay and prejudice]; Fisher v. Larsen (1982) 138 Cal.App.3d 627, 649 [affirming denial of amendment based on long delay].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
